seo.title: New Hampshire Dental Assistant License (2026) | Fees & Guide
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in New Hampshire — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license new hampshire, dental assistant certification new hampshire, RDA new hampshire, DANB CDA new hampshire, dental assistant radiography new hampshire

board.name: New Hampshire Board of Dental Examiners
board.mailing_address:
New Hampshire Board of Dental Examiners
Office of Professional Licensure and Certification
7 Eagle Square
Concord, NH 03301

license_types.title: Registered Dental Assistant
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in New Hampshire.

steps.title: Meet Eligibility Requirements
steps.description: Ensure you meet the state's requirements for an EFDA license, which include either graduation from a CODA-accredited dental assisting program or 3,000 hours of clinical dental assisting experience, a current DANB CDA or COA certification, and current healthcare provider BLS/CPR certification.

steps.title: Create an Account and Apply Online
steps.description: Visit the OPLC Online Application System, register for a new account, and then log in to select 'Start a New Application' for an Expanded Function Dental Auxiliary (EFDA) license.

steps.title: Complete and Submit Application
steps.description: Fill out the online application form completely, upload all required supporting documentation such as transcripts, certifications, and BLS/CPR. Pay the non-refundable $125 application fee through the OPLC Online Application System.

steps.title: Pass the NH EFDA Examination
steps.description: Upon Board approval of your application, schedule and successfully pass the New Hampshire Expanded Function Dental Auxiliary Examination. A separate examination fee of $200 will be required for this clinical exam.

steps.title: Receive Your License
steps.description: Once all application requirements, documentation, and examination results are verified and approved by the New Hampshire Board of Dental Examiners, your EFDA license will be issued and accessible via your OPLC account.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Registered Dental Assistant through the New Hampshire Board of Dental Examiners by registration.

faqs.question: Does New Hampshire require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, New Hampshire requires Registered Dental Assistant through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in New Hampshire?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in New Hampshire.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in New Hampshire?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in New Hampshire?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $50. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Registered Dental Assistant through the New Hampshire Board of Dental Examiners by registration.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in New Hampshire
hero.dek: To become a licensed Expanded Function Dental Auxiliary (EFDA) in New Hampshire, applicants must apply through the OPLC Online Application System, which handles all professional licensure for the state. New Hampshire does not license traditional dental assistants for basic duties.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the OPLC Online Application System page, click 'Register a New Account', and follow the prompts to create a user ID and password. Once registered and logged in, select 'Start a New Application' and choose 'Expanded Function Dental Auxiliary (EFDA)'.
